description_full:
Founded in 2017, refurbed is Europe’s fastest-growing marketplace for refurbished products, active in 12 European countries and having surpassed €2Bn in GMV — all while being profitable. With beautiful headquarters in Vienna, we operate as a remote-first company, and our 250+ employees enjoy up to two months of workation per year. We’ve also been recognised as a Top DACH Employer by Kununu for three consecutive years.




Our mission is to make sustainable consumption the easy choice by enabling customers to buy products up to 40% cheaper while significantly reducing CO₂ emissions.




If you thrive in an environment that values momentum, ownership, and impact, you’ll feel at home here. We’re a fast-paced, high-performance team that works hard and challenges itself everyday. To enable this high-performance every team member enjoys full autonomy over their location (we’re remote-first).




The Category and Sales Marketing Analyst will play a crucial role in supporting vouchers and promotion operations, multi-category growth, improving supply-demand alignment, and strengthening the commercial foundations needed for successful marketing strategies across multiple categories, markets, and performance channels.




This role will directly enable Refurbed’s expansion across categories, brands and countries by taking ownership of operational and analytical tasks.




You will work closely with Demand Planning, Category Managers, CRM, Performance Marketing (Search/Shopping, Paid Social, Affiliates), Product, and Commercial teams.




WHO YOU ARE
2–5 years experience in e-commerce, category management, marketing operations, performance marketing, or commercial strategy.
Strong analytical and data-handling skills; able to create clear insights from complex data.
Highly organized, reliable, and operationally strong.
Comfortable coordinating with multiple teams and managing parallel workstreams across categories, markets, and performance channels.
Proactive mindset with strong problem-solving skills.
Experience with consumer electronics / SDA is a plus.
Experience with promotions or marketplace operations preferred.
Bonus: performance marketing fundamentals (e.g., Search/Shopping, Paid Social, Affiliates)




Location & eligibility: This role is open to candidates based in Europe (including UK) only with a valid right to work.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

You drive multi-category growth. You support prioritization and budget allocation across categories and markets, turning demand-side insights (search, click data, pricing signals) into clear, actionable growth opportunities and structured recommendations.
You run promotions end-to-end. You execute voucher campaigns across markets, monitor performance and ROI, and continuously optimize based on supply relevance, cost efficiency, and commercial impact.
You connect Marketing and Supply. You align teams by consolidating forecasts, stock, pricing, and partner inputs, ensuring everyone works off the same priorities and timelines while contributing clear insights to business reviews.
You enable brands and partners. You maintain key KPIs, ensure data readiness for activation, and support smooth rollout of brand campaigns.
You scale how we work. You build tools, dashboards, and documentation that enable teams to be more self-serve, while identifying automation opportunities to reduce manual effort.
You support performance execution. You help translate category priorities into performance marketing channels (Search, Paid Social, Affiliates) and ensure campaigns are set up for success.




WHY YOU WILL ENJOY WORKING WITH US:

Our Culture and Values:

Decisions should be made based on facts and not by hierarchy levels? We sure think so!
We embrace giving and receiving feedback, testing and welcoming new ideas, as well as sharing our knowledge across the company.
You fail sometimes? Well, so do we! We’re all just human, let’s learn from our mistakes to improve in the future!
No matter who you love, where you’re from, who you pray to, whether you pee sitting down or standing up – we are a bunch of talented people who enjoy spending our time making a difference!

Personal & Professional Development:

You'll have access to a dedicated learning budget within your department to support your growth, whether it's courses, conferences, or other learning opportunities that matter to you.
We partner with Likeminded, offering you private sessions with experts for your personal growth and mental health.
We frequently host internal workshops, allowing our teams to further develop their skills in different areas and enhancing collaboration across departments.

Flexibility:

We care about a healthy work/life balance. We not only function and support a fully remote setting but also offer the possibility to work from our beautiful office in the vibrant heart of Vienna – we love dogs, so feel free to bring your furry little buddy too 😊
We trust you to do the best job, so we don't care so much when you do it – you are able to self-organize.
You will also be invited to take part in our office weeks, happening three times a year, where you will have the chance to work side by side with your colleagues and enjoy the team-building events and on-site workshops




…and much more! See https://careers.refurbed.com/ for further insights!

description_full:
About Agoda

At Agoda, we bridge the world through travel. Our story began in 2005, when two lifelong friends and entrepreneurs, driven by their passion for travel, launched Agoda to make it easier for everyone to explore the world.

Today, we are part of Booking Holdings [NASDAQ: BKNG], with a diverse team of over 7,000 people from 90 countries, working together in offices around the globe. Every day, we connect people to destinations and experiences, with our great deals across our millions of hotels and holiday properties, flights, and experiences worldwide.

No two days are the same at Agoda. Data and technology are at the heart of our culture, fueling our curiosity and innovation. If you’re ready to begin your best journey and help build travel for the world, join us.

Get to know our team:

The Marketing team at Agoda is highly data-driven and focused on developing at-scale, measurable marketing programs designed to improve the lifetime value of Agoda customers. The team combines a blend of the best analysts, marketing strategists and data scientists, collectively building a team that thrives on data, creative ideas and technology. . The Marketing leadership fosters a great learning environment, providing the opportunity to learn and grow by working closely with experts from a diverse variety of backgrounds from across the globe.

Due to continued expansion, there are multiple analyst roles within the marketing team across different marketing channels. While the scope will vary depending on these channels, here are some examples:


Experimentation and optimizing campaign performance: Experiment with ads/campaign structures and bidding& pricing strategies on partners such as Google, Bing, Tripadvisor, Trivago and other search engines. Adapt to new product features and roll out changes from successful tests.
Modeling: Analyze vast amounts of data generated by experiments, develop predictive models using data science techniques (eg. understanding impact on bookings from large scale TV campaigns or demand elasticity from pricing optimization) and liaise with product teams on implementation roadmap
Reporting, analysis and insights: Building dashboards to track performance, derive insights, understand growth levers and communicate recommendations via presentations to stakeholders


What you’ll Need to Succeed:


Bachelor’s degree or higher from top university in a quantitative subject (computer science, mathematics, engineering business or science)
Excellent verbal and written communication skills in English
2+ years experience in data crunching from top-tier consulting, investment banking, private equity or strategy/business for a fast-growing globaltech company
Experience in one or more data analysis packages or databases, eg., SAS, R, SPSS, Python, VBA, SQL and visualization tools, eg. Tableau, Power BI
Ability to move fast and be efficient, making decisions on objective evidence rather than subjective gut feel
Innate desire to take ownership, make an impact and influence outcomes
Excellent organizational skills, attention to detail and ability to work independently


It’s great if you have:


Experience in digital marketing or ecommerce
Experience with A/B testing and other testing metrics
Strong presentation and negotiation skills
Skills that foster collaboration and team spirit

description_full:
Vollgas nicht nur auf der Straße – sondern auch mit uns im Job!

Die Stellantis Germany GmbH ist Teil des weltweiten Automobilkonzerns Stellantis N.V.

In Deutschland ist die Stellantis Germany GmbH aus den beiden Unternehmensteilen

FCA Germany GmbH und Groupe PSA Deutschland GmbH hervorgegangen. Unter dem Dach von Stellantis werden die Marken Abarth, Alfa Romeo, Citroën, DS Automobiles, Fiat, Fiat Professional (leichte Nutzfahrzeuge), Jeep®, Leapmotor, Opel und Peugeot vertrieben.




Der Market Funnel Analyst (m/w/d) unterstützt die tägliche Umsetzung des Monitorings, der Diagnose und der Optimierung der Funnel-Performance.

Die Rolle nutzt regionale Dashboards, KPIs und Measurement-Frameworks und wendet zugleich marktspezifisches Know-how an, um Daten korrekt zu interpretieren und umsetzbare Optimierungsmaßnahmen zu empfehlen.

Diese Rolle stellt sicher, dass das regionale Reporting im Markt konsistent genutzt wird, und reichert es durch lokale Insights, Nuancen und Performance-Learnings an.




1. Funnel-Monitoring mit regionalen Dashboards

Performance über alle Funnel-Stufen hinweg mithilfe regionaler Tools tracken.
Anomalien, Trends, Wachstumschancen und Drop-offs identifizieren.
Performance anhand lokaler Channel-, Campaign- und Zielgruppen-Insights kontextualisieren.




2. Insight-Entwicklung für lokale Governance & regionale Abstimmung

Funnel-Insight-Summaries für Markt-Performance-Routinen vorbereiten.
Klare, kontextualisierte Erklärungen zu KPI-Bewegungen liefern.
Lokale Insights zu regionalen Reports und Playbooks beitragen.




3. Umsetzung von Optimierungen

Die Implementierung von Optimierungsmaßnahmen über Media, CRM und Content hinweg unterstützen.
A/B-Tests gemäß regionalen Frameworks durchführen, angepasst an Marktspezifika.
Test-Performance tracken und Learnings dokumentieren.




4. Reporting-Disziplin & Datenqualität

Sicherstellen, dass der Markt auf regionale Dashboards setzt (kein Parallel-Reporting).
Datenqualität validieren und Issues an regionale und lokale Owner eskalieren.
Konsistente Anwendung regionaler KPI-Definitionen sicherstellen.




5. Zusammenarbeit & Support

Eng mit CRM-, Media-, Market- und Brand-Teams zusammenarbeiten, damit Funnel-KPIs Ausführungsentscheidungen steuern.
Vorbereitung von Markt- und regionalen Performance-Reviews unterstützen.
Bei KPI-Schwankungen Deep-Dive-Diagnosen bereitstellen.




Unterstützte zentrale Funnel-KPIs

Awareness: Reichweite, CPR, Traffic-Qualität
Consideration: CTR, Engagement, Lead-Volumen, CPL
Intent & Conversion: Lead-to-Sale-Conversion, CPA
Retention: Loyalty-Rate, CRM-Lifecycle-KPIs
Effizienz: Effizienz der Budgetallokation
Forecasting: Prognosegenauigkeit (Demand/Leads/Sales)




Skills & Kompetenzen

Ausgeprägte analytische Fähigkeiten und hohe Detailgenauigkeit
Fähigkeit, Funnel-KPIs und Performance-Signale zu interpretieren
Sicherer Umgang mit Analytics-Plattformen (PowerBI, GA4, CRM-Tools)
Klare Kommunikation und Storytelling
Sicheres Arbeiten in strukturierten Frameworks
Problemlösungsorientiertes Mindset




Erfahrung

1–4 Jahre Erfahrung in Digital Analytics, Media Performance, CRM-Analytics oder Marketing Operations
Vorherige Erfahrung mit Funnel-KPIs wünschenswert
Erfahrung im Umgang mit standardisierten Reporting-Umgebungen







Interesse geweckt? Wir freuen uns auf Ihre Bewerbung!

Sie erwartet eine attraktive und herausfordernde Tätigkeit eines dynamischen und internationalen Automobilkonzerns. Wenn Sie sich durch unsere Stellenausschreibung angesprochen fühlen, dann freuen wir uns auf Ihre aussagekräftige Bewerbung (Motivationsschreiben, Lebenslauf, Zeugnisse) unter Angabe Ihres frühestmöglichen Eintrittstermins und Gehaltswunsches.

Über WERTGARANTIE Group

Der starke Arbeitgeber im Herzen Hannovers

Wer wir sind? Ein Unternehmen auf Wachstumskurs in einer Branche mit Zukunft. Wir bieten vielfältige und abwechslungsreiche Aufgaben sowie tolle Leistungen für unsere Mitarbeitenden. Als Lifestyle-Versicherer schützen wir neben Konsumelektronik, Haushaltsgeräten, Fahrrädern, E-Bikes und E-Scootern sowie Hörgeräten auch Smart Home-Anlagen und Hausleitungen. Unsere Mission: Reparieren statt Wegwerfen – eine geniale Idee, nachhaltig gut.

Was bieten wir dir?


Attraktive Vergütung: Du erhältst ein faires Gehalt, das durch jährliche Urlaubs- und Weihnachtsgeldzahlungen ergänzt wird
Umfangreiche Urlaubstage: Du genießt 30 Tage Urlaub pro Jahr, plus einen zusätzlichen Gesundheitstag, der wie ein Urlaubstag genutzt werden kann
Nachhaltige Mobilität und Boni: Du profitierst von einem erfolgsbasierten und nachhaltigen Bonussystem sowie nachhaltiger Mobilität
Individuelle Förderung: Nutze unsere vielfältigen Angebote, um dich persönlich und fachlich weiterzuentwickeln
Gesundheitsförderung: Wir stärken dich und deine Gesundheit mit Hansefit, Massagen und Sportevents
Wertschätzung und Teamgeist: Wir nutzen jede Gelegenheit, um gemeinsam unsere kleinen und großen Erfolge zu feiern


Was erwartet dich?


Du analysierst Kunden-, Nutzungs- und Kampagnendaten und leitest daraus konkrete, operative Insights ab
Du entwickelst und setzt Zielgruppenanalysen, Selektionen und Segmentierungen um
Du bewertest die Wirkung unserer Kommunikations- und Direktmarketingmaßnahmen anhand relevanter Kennzahlen
Du entwickelst Selektionslogiken, Modelle und Algorithmen kontinuierlich weiter
Du erstellst aussagekräftige Reportings und Präsentationen zur Kampagnen- und Kommunikationsperformance und ordnest diese in übergeordnete Customer-Experience- und Wirkungszusammenhänge ein


Was solltest du mitbringen?


Du hast ein abgeschlossenes Studium im wirtschaftswissenschaftlichen, betriebswirtschaftlichen oder vergleichbaren Bereich
Du hast Erfahrung im Bereich Analytics, Audience Management, Marketing Analytics oder in vergleichbaren Themenfeldern
Du bringst ein sehr gutes Zahlenverständnis mit und hast Freude daran, analytisch und strukturiert zu arbeiten
Du interessierst dich für Customer Experience, Kundenkommunikation und datenbasierte Steuerung
Du kannst Analyseergebnisse verständlich aufbereiten und in konkrete Handlungsempfehlungen übersetzen
Du gehst sicher mit MS Office sowie gängigen Analyse- und Marketing-Tools um (z. B. BSI, Formware, Datenbank- oder BI-Tools) und bist offen für den Einsatz KI-gestützter Analyseverfahren sowie neuer datengetriebener Technologien
Nach Eingang deiner Bewerbung wird diese vom Recruiting und vom Fachbereich geprüft
Sollte deine Bewerbung uns überzeugen, erhältst du eine Einladung zu einem Videointerview
Nach positiven Erstinterview wirst du zu einem Zweitgespräch/Hospitation vor Ort eingeladen
Ist auch dieses Gespräch positiv verlaufen, erhältst du ein Gehaltsangebot von uns

Are you passionate about shaping data-driven pricing strategies on a global scale? At HUGO BOSS, we are seeking a highly skilled BP&A Pricing Expert (m/f/d) to drive impactful pricing decisions across all brands, product categories, and channels. In this role, you will partner closely with top management, steer strategic pricing and budgeting processes, and evaluate new business opportunities. Join us in elevating pricing excellence and performance transparency in a dynamic, fast-paced, and international environment.

The position is limited to a fixed term of 2 years.

What you can expect:


Serve as the primary BP&A contact for all pricing-related topics within the global HUGO BOSS pricing strategy across all brands, product categories, and sales channels
Drive price adjustments by analyzing underlying data, conducting impact assessments, and preparing clear decision-making and action recommendations for top management
Further develop and actively shape the budgeting process, including the preparation of key budget rounds, ensuring alignment with strategic company objectives as well as dynamic market and competitive conditions
Contribute to the design and development of business cases to evaluate new brand partnerships and product initiatives
Analyze, prepare, and interpret relevant performance KPIs to assess the effectiveness and efficiency of pricing, marketing, and individual business unit performance



Your profile:


Minimum of 3 years of professional experience in Controlling, Business Development, Brand Management, Marketing, or a comparable function
Strong analytical skills combined with innovative thinking and creativity in tackling complex business challenges
Fluency in English (written and spoken) and excellent communication skills, enabling effective collaboration with stakeholders at different levels
A collaborative team player with a hands-on mentality, strong organizational skills, and a high degree of ownership and accountability
High digital affinity with a strong focus on leveraging digital tools and technologies to drive efficiency and improve processes



Your benefits:


We offer a competitive salary and attractive benefits, including vacation and Christmas bonuses, a mobility allowance / Deutschlandticket, an annual clothing allowance, a company pension scheme, and our SHIP share program.
Made for Me: Enjoy the freedom of our flexible working models. With 'Threedom of Work', spend 3 days in Metzingen and 2 days working remotely. Benefit from our 'Me Time' program for personal time off and the option of 30 days of 'Mobile Work EU' annually.
High-performing people need a healthy balance. Take advantage of the employee gym, a variety of sports activities, a 37-hour workweek, and 30 vacation days a year.
Exclusive discounts for shopping and arts: benefit from discounts for family and friends along with free entrance to more than 15 international art museums.
Does innovation drive you? Same for us! This passion is reflected in our modern workplaces and innovative tools.
Sustainability is one of our key values, and more than just a trend. We are committed to environmental, animal and climate protection, and human rights. As part of our commitment, we offer an environmentally friendly bus shuttle.
We also place great importance to good taste when it comes to food. Welcome to our award-winning restaurant & café Times.



We are a global company with our employees representative of the world at large. Our inclusive culture embraces each person’s authenticity and individuality. We are committed to equal employment opportunity. And we believe our equitable work environment helps unleash your full potential and inspires you to thrive.

description_full:
At Statista, we’re all about facts and data, for we are the world's leading business data platform. By providing reliable and easy-to-use data as well as various data analytics products and services, we empower people worldwide to make fact-based decisions.

Founded in Hamburg in 2007, we have quickly grown into a global company with offices in major cities such as London, New York, Berlin and Tokyo. And we still have a lot of plans. Our constant growth does not only prove our success, but also keeps creating new development and career opportunities for our employees.

We value and celebrate our diverse culture. You are welcome here for who you are, no matter where you come from, what you look like, or whether you prefer bar graphs to pie charts. Your story matters – keep writing it as part of our team.

Are you ready to join us?

Your Team

At Statista R, our team of over 130 analysts and sales specialists creates, publishes and distributes globally renowned company and brand toplists as well as rankings for a wide range of industries. As experts in rating companies, brands and products, we use our independent market research and data analysis expertise to identify the best in their respective industries, creating transparency and trust. Learn more at r.statista.com.

Your Job


Contribute to multifaceted international media cooperation projects
Analyze e-commerce companies and online platforms, including their business models and performance metrics
Research and analyze internal and external primary and secondary data to assess company performance and develop rankings
Support the creation of international marketing campaigns in the rankings environment
Assist in planning and executing company outreach campaigns (e.g. email campaigns)
Prepare clear and structured presentations of results in PowerPoint and Excel
Collaborate with the team to develop the best approaches for our projects


Your Profile


Completed studies in economics, business administration, marketing or a related field (Bachelor/Master)
Strong interest in e-commerce markets, digital platforms and online business models
First practical experience in marketing, outreach or campaign management (e.g. internships, student jobs or early professional experience)
Experience with email campaigns, mass mailings or direct company outreach is a strong plus
Very good knowledge of Excel and PowerPoint; experience with data tools or programming (e.g. Python, R or SQL) is a plus
Excellent written and spoken English; German or additional languages are a plus
High level of initiative, reliability and attention to detail, with the ability to work both independently and in a team


What We Offer

In addition to our great team, culture, and our shared goal of empowering people with data, there are many other things that make Statista a great place to work! Join us and benefit from:


Work from abroad up to 30 calendar days a year
Hybrid work and flex-time
International team and social events
Subsidized urban mobility and access to fitness and wellness options
Free access to Langdock and all its amazing functionalities
Career & training opportunities
Attractive locations and modern offices
Mental health support with OpenUp

description_full:
Die Koordination der EDEKA-Strategie erfolgt in der Hamburger EDEKA-Zentrale. Sie steuert das nationale Warengeschäft ebenso wie die erfolgreiche Kampagne „Wir lieben Lebensmittel“ und gibt vielfältige Impulse zur Realisierung verbundübergreifender Ziele. Mit dem Tochterunternehmen Netto Marken-Discount setzt sie darüber hinaus erfolgreiche Akzente im Discountgeschäft.

EDEKA erzielte 2025 mit rund 10.900 Märkten und rund 417.500 Mitarbeitenden einen Umsatz von 77,3 Mrd. Euro. Mit rund 20.900 Auszubildenden ist EDEKA einer der führenden Ausbilder in Deutschland.




Business Analyst bei EDEKA – Deine Chance, den Lebensmittelhandel neu zu denken

Als Business Analyst  im digitalen Marketing bei EDEKA stehst du im Herzen unserer digitalen Transformation. Du arbeitest an Themen, die Millionen Kund:innen täglich berühren – von der App‑Experience bis zu datengetriebenen Loyalty‑Mechaniken. Dein Impact ist unmittelbar sichtbar: Du identifizierst Chancen, entwickelst Hypothesen, bringst Innovationen voran und gestaltest aktiv die Zukunft des Einkaufens.

Bei uns hast du die Möglichkeit, anspruchsvolle Herausforderungen mit analytischem Scharfsinn, Kreativität und Ownership anzugehen. Dein Weg ist nicht vorgegeben – du formst ihn selbst, unterstützt von starken Teams aus CRM, App, IT und Marketing.



Deine Aufgaben



Kunden verstehen – Erlebnisse gestalten

Du analysierst Transaktions‑, Response‑ und Interaktionsdaten, um tiefgreifende Erkenntnisse über Kundenverhalten, Angebotswirkung und Aktivierungsmechaniken zu gewinnen.
Du entwickelst unsere digitalen Kundeninteraktionen – insbesondere in der EDEKA App – sowie Loyalty‑Programme kontinuierlich weiter.

Hypothesen testen – Wirkung maximieren

Du entwickelst, testest und validierst Hypothesen, um datenbasierte, umsetzbare und skalierbare Maßnahmen abzuleiten.
Du konzipierst, führst durch und analysierst A/B‑ und multivariate Tests, um Personalisierung, CRM und Angebotskommunikation messbar zu verbessern.

Innovation treiben – Lösungen in die Realität bringen

Du gestaltest datenbasierte Lösungen, die Kundenbindung stärken, Angebotsrelevanz erhöhen und das Nutzererlebnis spürbar verbessern.
Du identifizierst Optimierungs‑ und Innovationspotenziale entlang der gesamten Customer Journey – digital wie analog.

End‑to‑End‑Ownership übernehmen

Du arbeitest ganzheitlich: von der Analyse über die Konzeption bis zur Umsetzung mit den relevanten Fachbereichen.
Du übersetzt komplexe Sachverhalte in klare, präzise Entscheidungsgrundlagen – für operative Teams ebenso wie für strategische Stakeholder.


Dein Profil



Fachliche Skills & Erfahrung

Erfahrung mit Performance‑Daten, E‑Commerce, Datenmanagement oder Commercial Analytics.
Solide Kenntnisse in SQL, Excel, Power BI und Python.
Technologieverständnis zu digitalen Architekturen, Softwareentwicklung und datengetriebenen Produkten.
Sicherer Umgang mit Tools wie Jira, Confluence oder GitHub.
Ein Studium in (Wirtschafts‑)Informatik, Data Science, Digital Business, Mathematik, E‑Commerce, Wirtschaftspsychologie o. Ä.
Berufserfahrung im Handel, Start‑up oder Tech‑Umfeld – idealerweise mehrjährig.

Arbeitsweise & Denkstil

Du denkst analytisch, strukturiert und liebst es, komplexe Zusammenhänge zu durchdringen.
Du arbeitest ergebnisorientiert, pragmatisch und mit hohem Qualitätsanspruch.
Du kannst klare Hypothesen formulieren, Entscheidungen ableiten und Empfehlungen geben.
Du übernimmst Ownership und findest Wege statt Hürden.

Kommunikation & Zusammenarbeit

Du arbeitest gern in interdisziplinären Teams und vermittelst souverän zwischen Business & IT.
Du kannst datengetriebene Insights präzise und verständlich präsentieren.
Du bringst Engagement, Eigeninitiative und Verantwortungsbewusstsein mit.
Du verfügst über sehr gute Deutsch‑ und Englischkenntnisse.


Unser Angebot


Ein sicherer Arbeitsplatz in einem sehr erfolgreichen Handelsunternehmen mit Sitz in Hamburg, City Nord
Wir bieten flexible Arbeitszeiten, die Möglichkeit zum anteiligen mobilen Arbeiten und 30 Tage Urlaub
Individuelle Weiterbildungsmöglichkeiten und Karrierechancen sind durch unsere internen Entwicklungsprogramme gewährleistet
Es erwartet dich täglich ein frisch gekochtes Mittagessen in unserer Kantine zu attraktiven Preisen
Ein leistungsgerechtes Vergütungspaket und vielfältige weitere Benefits, wie z.B. Fitnessstudio, Concierge-Service, Zuschuss zum Deutschlandticket, Dienstfahrrad, Vorteile bei zahlreichen Partnerunternehmen oder betriebliche Altersvorsorge sind für uns selbstverständlich
Daneben bieten wir auch ein umfangreiches und prämiertes Gesundheitsmanagement


Interessiert?



Dann freuen wir uns auf Deine Online-Bewerbung mit aussagekräftigen Bewerbungsunterlagen (Lebenslauf und vollständige Zeugnisse) unter Angabe Deiner Gehaltsvorstellung und Deines möglichen Eintrittstermins.

Willkommen sind bei uns alle Menschen – unabhängig von Geschlecht, Nationalität, ethnischer und sozialer Herkunft, Behinderung, Religion, Alter sowie sexueller Orientierung.



Kontakt



Herr Andre Jelken

Melde Dich gerne bei mir, wenn Du Fragen zum Bewerbungsprozess oder zur Stelle hast: +49 40 6377 2536

Egal, in welchem Team in unserem Unternehmen: Jede*r Einzelne ist fester Teil einer großen Idee, eines Umbruchs und Teil eines der spannendsten und zukunftsweisendsten Themen unserer Zeit: Der Energie, die uns alle voranbringt.

Wir laden Deutschland auf. Und alle ein.

Change the way the world travels

Join the GetYourGuide journey to connect people with unforgettable travel experiences around the world. Millions look to us for unique activities they can trust, and it’s all powered by our commitment to make every single journey extraordinary - including yours.

Ready to unlock your potential with a community of fellow explorers? Find your next role at our Berlin HQ or one of our local offices around the globe, from New York to Bangkok. Head to getyourguide.careers to take the first step.

Team mission

The Analytics department’s mission is to enable the Marketing organisation to take action based on data. We work on a variety of problems and datasets as members of cross-functional mission teams. We take ownership of the entire analytics funnel, from exploration through recommendations to impact measurement, and take pride in the insights we generate.

This specific position is within our Marketing Analytics team whose objective is to help our marketing scale our investments towards the most incremental and loyal customers. This is a very strategic position within the team as the Analyst will look at solving cross-channel problems in order to accelerate our growth. This means for example addressing the best strategy to grow our display and mid-funnel spend and understanding the behaviour of our repeat audiences.

Your mission


Develop quantitative analysis and statistical models to evaluate and steer marketing decisions
Propose and defend strategic recommendations to our top marketing leads
Setup and analyse marketing experiments to drive strategic decision making
Explore large datasets, give meaning to data and numbers, identify opportunities and issues, and present findings to stakeholders
Maintain and improve our marketing measurement tools (Attribution, experimentation framework, Customer lifetime value…)
Serve as a liaison between the marketing teams and the data engineering team
Mentor others in the skills needed for this role


Your toolkit


8+ years of relevant experience interpreting data in an analytics, data science or business intelligence role
Strong knowledge of SQL and Python for analysis (eg, pandas, scikit-learn, pyspark)
Ability to understand user behavior and meaning behind the numbers and a track record in sharing insights to business stakeholders
Prior exposure to digital and performance marketing or SEO, especially data-driven platforms such as Google Ads and Meta.
Experience with data visualization and reporting tools (e.g. Looker, Jupyter Notebooks, Databricks)
Desire to work in an international environment, with minimal direction and with highly engaged individuals
Excellent written and verbal communication skills in English


Extras that give you an edge


A track record in solving marketing and growth challenges and influencing decisions/driving business results
Experience in understanding and modelling marketing journeys
Experience with big data tools (e.g. Spark, Hive) is a plus

description_full:
Our Story

Every year millions of people are either filing their taxes in fear or giving up on their tax refund altogether. We're working on fixing that. Our intuitive app enables anyone, regardless of education or background, to file their taxes with newfound confidence.

Spread across Germany, Spain and the UK, the team at Taxfix Group with its brands Taxfix, Steuerbot and TaxScouts, is a compassionate group of solution-finders. We speak our minds openly, and with over 400 professionals, including tax experts, developers, and IT security experts, we're rich in ideas and voices. The group has facilitated more than 3.5 billion euros in tax refunds for its customers since its founding in 2016.

Your Challenge

Join our Marketing Analytics team as a Data Analyst, where your mission is to transform data into strategies that fuel growth. You'll apply advanced analytics and AI to influence CLV, CAC, and revenue outcomes. As a trusted partner to Growth Marketing, CRM, and MarTech, you’ll play a pivotal role in shaping how we scale efficiently in competitive markets.

Your Responsibilities And Decisions


Design, run, and improve measurement frameworks (attribution, MMM, incrementality tests, brand trackers). Use their insights to drive test-and-learn plans and guide strategic decisions on budgeting, channel mix, and retention.
Apply advanced statistical and predictive techniques, turning complex data into clear recommendations that directly influence marketing decisions to maximize CLV and reduce CAC.
Partner closely with Growth Marketing, MarTech, CRM, Finance, and Analytics Engineering to ensure data quality, model accuracy, and scalable implementation of insights.
Translate analytical findings into compelling narratives, dashboards, notebooks, decks, and recommendations, that drive decisions across Marketing.
Communicate insights clearly and persuasively, driving alignment across stakeholders and ensuring data-driven decisions are widely adopted.
Build and utilize AI-native analytics workflows (e.g. agentic data exploration, LLM-assisted causal analysis) and bring them into team practice.


Your Profile


2+ years in Marketing Analytics and Data Modeling, ideally in B2C acquisition and retention.
Hands-on with attribution models, MMM, incrementality testing and campaign performance analysis across digital paid channels (search, social, programmatic) and CRM. Exposure to offline/brand measurement (TV, CTV, podcast) is beneficial.
Hands-on expertise in statistical modeling and machine learning (e.g. regression, Bayesian methods, classification, clustering, forecasting), with experience in causal inference and experimentation design applied to marketing use cases such as CLV prediction, demand forecasting and pricing elasticity models.
Advanced SQL for data manipulation, Python for statistical analysis and predictive modeling, experience with dbt for data modeling and Looker (or similar BI tools) for dashboarding and reporting.
Strong ability to extract insights and connect them to CAC, CLV, and retention outcomes, guiding acquisition and retention strategies.
Skilled at distilling complex data into actionable insights for both technical and non-technical stakeholders, with experience influencing senior leaders.
Proactive, outcome-driven, and comfortable owning analyses from data pipeline to business recommendation that drives measurable business impact.
Practical experience applying AI-driven solutions such as prototyping with LLMs (e.g. Claude Code, Gemini CLI), automating workflows (e.g. LangChain, n8n), using AI-powered IDEs (e.g. Cursor) to accelerate coding and analysis, or enhancing marketing analytics processes.


Why Taxfix?


A chance to do meaningful, people-centric work with an international team of passionate professionals.
Holistic well-being with free mental health coaching sessions and yoga.
A monthly allowance to spend on an extensive range of services that you can use and roll over as flexibly as you like.
Employee stock options for all employees—because everyone deserves to benefit from the success they help to create.
30 annual vacation days and flexible working hours.
Work from abroad for up to six weeks every year. Just align with your team, and then enjoy your trip.
Plenty of opportunities to socialise as a team. In addition to internal tech meetups, our international team hosts regular get-togethers—virtually and in person when possible.
Free tax declaration filing, of course, through the Taxfix app—and internal support for all personal tax-related questions.
Have a four-legged friend in your life? We’re happy to have dogs join us in the office.


Excited? So are we. Learn more about Team Taxfix on our blog and get a glimpse of our culture.

At Taxfix, we believe that incredible things happen when you have a wealth of perspectives and experiences. We're proudly committed to equal employment and development opportunities no matter your gender, race, religion, age, sexual orientation, colour, disability, or place of origin. To help mitigate any potential unconscious biases, we ask that you refrain from including your picture, age, or marital status on your CV. Let your experiences speak for themselves.

Not sure if you meet all the requirements for this role? Please apply anyway. You might bring something special to the team that we hadn't considered previously.

description_full:
Help Us Change the Way the World Works

Build something that matters.

Langdock exists to change the way the world works, bridging the gap between what technology can do and what people actually do with it. We bring all leading AI models into one secure, model-agnostic platform and make them usable across entire organizations. Over 6,000 companies use our platform every day, from fast-growing startups to some of Europe's largest enterprises. Their employees open Langdock to draft strategies, analyze documents, or automate workflows - helping them to work smarter, think more creatively, and reach their full potential.

What "Product Marketer" Means Here

We ship constantly. New features, new capabilities, new integrations. Most of it does not get the attention it deserves, not because it is not good, but because translating what shipped into why it matters is genuinely hard work, and it requires someone who sits between product and market full time.

That is this role. You are the person who understands what we are building deeply enough to explain it clearly, and who understands our customers well enough to know which parts they care about. You turn releases into stories. You turn capabilities into positioning. You make sure what we build reaches the people who need it.

What You Will Actually Do


Own go-to-market for product releases. Every meaningful feature that ships needs a plan: who needs to know, what they need to hear, and how it reaches them. You build that plan and execute it across the right channels.
Develop and maintain positioning. What is Langdock? What makes it different? Why does it matter to a head of IT at a German Mittelstand company versus an AI lead at a fast-growing startup? You develop the answers and keep them sharp as the product and market evolve.
Create content that explains the product. Comparison pages, capability overviews, one-pagers for sales, explainers for campaigns. You write clearly about complex things and you know when to use a diagram instead of a paragraph.
Work with customers to understand what they value. The best product marketing comes from listening. You run conversations, read support tickets, and sit in on sales calls to stay close to how customers actually talk about the product.
Enable sales and customer success. You make sure the people talking to customers every day have what they need: the right framing, the right materials, the right answers to the hard questions.


What Makes This Different

Product marketing at most companies is reactive. A feature ships, and then someone writes a blog post. Here it is a core function. The product is moving fast, the market is moving fast, and the gap between what we can do and what people know we can do is real.

You will also work on positioning for a category that is still being defined. Enterprise AI adoption is not a mature market with established playbooks. The way you frame Langdock now shapes how people think about the category.

And because we are small, you will see the impact of your work directly. A repositioned feature that starts converting better. A one-pager that sales starts pulling into every deal. A capability overview that a customer forwards to their board.

You Might Be a Fit If...


You understand technology well enough to explain it simply. You do not need to be an engineer, but you need to be able to sit in a product review and understand what is being built and why.
You write clearly. Not cleverly, clearly. The best product marketing rarely sounds clever. It sounds obvious in retrospect.
You are genuinely curious about customers. You find it interesting to understand how a company works, what their AI journey looks like, and where they get stuck. You use that to make the marketing better.
You have done this before in some form. A launch you ran, a positioning exercise you led, messaging that worked. You can talk about it concretely.
You use AI tools as a genuine part of how you work. Research, drafting, synthesis. You are fast because you have figured out how to be.
You would rather own a problem than be handed a brief.
You are a kind person who cares about the people around you.


The Environment

We work from our office in Berlin, Greifswalder Strasse 212. Everyone works together in person because the hardest problems get solved faster at a whiteboard than in a Slack thread. Conversations happen faster, problems get solved quicker, and we actually know each other.

Days start at 8:30. Lunch & dinner are together. We run, go to the gym, and take care of ourselves. Health is not separate from work here, it is part of how we work well.

The vibe is calm but intense. No one is yelling or panicking. But everyone is working hard on things that matter.

Compensation

Salaries are transparent and tied to levels, not negotiation. All roles include equity.

We will figure out the right level together based on your experience and scope. Levels are about the work you own, not your title or years of experience. We narrow down the expected salary range early in the process.

Next steps

We move fast. Most processes complete within two weeks.

If this sounds like your kind of work, we would like to meet you.

description_full:
About Us

Established in March 2018, Bybit is one of the fastest growing cryptocurrency derivatives exchanges, with more than 70 million registered users. We offer a professional platform where crypto traders can find an ultra-fast matching engine, excellent customer service and multilingual community support. We provide innovative online spot and derivatives trading services, mining and staking products, as well as API support, to retail and institutional clients around the world, and strive to be the most reliable exchange for the emerging digital asset class.




Our core values define us. We listen, care, and improve to create a faster, fairer, and more humane trading environment for our users. Our innovative, highly advanced, user-friendly platform has been designed from the ground-up using best-in-class infrastructure to provide our users with the industry's safest, fastest, fairest, and most transparent trading experience. Built on customer-centric values, we endeavour to provide a professional, 24/7 multi-language customer support to help in a timely manner.




As of today, Bybit is one of the most trusted, reliable, and transparent cryptocurrency derivatives platforms in the space.




Role Overview

We’re looking for a passionate and organized Marketing Specialist to join the Payments Business Unit at Bybit. You’ll play an active role in shaping and executing marketing campaigns across regions, helping bring Bybit’s payment products to a global audience. If you’re eager to learn, comfortable with fast-paced projects, and curious about the intersection of crypto and fintech, this is the perfect opportunity to grow your skills.




Key Responsibilities

Support the creation, setup, and execution of marketing campaigns (including landing pages, voucher systems, and tracking setup).
Prepare and analyze campaign performance reports to provide insights and recommendations.
Assist in drafting creative briefs for video, social media, and event content.
Coordinate asset requests and timelines with the creative team.
Conduct content quality assurance (QA) before campaign launches.
Support marketing documentation, filing, and internal process organization.
Assist the partnership and events team with logistics and on-site execution of marketing activations.




What You Bring Along

Currently pursuing or recently completed a Master’s degree in Business, Marketing, Finance, or related disciplines.
Interest and knowledge of the crypto industry, digital payments, and financial technology.
Excellent organizational and multitasking abilities, with strong attention to detail.
Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and/or Google Sheets for data handling.
Basic understanding of digital marketing principles (tracking, analytics, social media, campaign performance).
Strong communication skills and a collaborative, proactive mindset.
Ability to adapt in a fast-paced, international environment.




Why Join Us

At Bybit, we are committed to fostering a supportive and enriching work environment.

Our benefits include:

- Study Growth Fund: We support your professional development and continuous learning.

- Internal Events: Participate in regular team-building activities, workshops, and events designed to promote collaboration and innovation.

- Global Collaboration: Be part of a diverse, international team, working alongside colleagues from around the world.

- Career Advancement: Access opportunities for growth and advancement within a rapidly expanding global company.

- Internal Mobility: Grow with us- Your long-term development is important to us. We offer internal job opportunities to help build your career path.

Description

In this role, you will be responsible for collaborating closely with our HQ Product Lines to drive the successful localization of the category across the EMEA region. You will drive the regional product marketing strategy, ensure seamless product launches, localize category storytelling, and optimize process/outcome metrics to achieve commercial success across European sub-countries.







What you will do

Collaborate closely with HQ product lines to localize category storytelling, and marketing strategies. Ensure the global product marketing strategy is seamlessly and efficiently implemented across various EMEA countries to support regional business performance.

University degree, ideally in Marketing or similar

Experience in being adaptable and a clear drive to meet deadlines, targets and budgets

The
description_full:
When travelers are searching for a hotel, we want the obvious choice to be trivago! Our leading metasearch engine is super fast and constantly optimized - enabling millions of travelers to compare hotel prices from hundreds of booking sites and find great deals in just a few clicks. We use cutting-edge technology, real-time auction, and machine learning techniques with petabytes of data to create an experience - time and money saved! In the lively city of Düsseldorf, we seize opportunities to learn everyday, innovate, and make an enduring mark on the travel industry. At trivago you will find those who aren’t afraid of change but rather embrace it, turning every challenge into a pathway for growth. Join trivago, work with a great team, and grow with us!

Join us in making a difference


The Marketing Strategy and Intelligence team at trivago is a unique blend of business and data roles, driving strategic decisions in marketing through data insights, innovative products, and deep market understanding. As the Strategy and Growth Manager, you will be dedicated to uncovering new growth opportunities and delivering business value through financial planning, controlling, performance and opportunity analysis, as well as project management and execution of strategic cross-functional initiatives.

In this role, you will collaborate with data and business experts on projects that impact every aspect of trivago's operations.

How you’ll make an impact:



Financial Management & Performance Optimization: Drive financial modeling and planning across markets — building robust models, leading marketing P&L forecasting and controlling processes, and continuously identifying optimization opportunities to maximize revenue and contribution margin growth.
Opportunity Identification & Validation: Collaborate closely with marketing teams to identify and define growth opportunities, build supporting business cases, and validate hypotheses through structured experimentation and testing frameworks.
Strategic Coordination: Drive strategic initiatives end-to-end, partnering with Business Units on strategy formation and quarterly objective-setting by providing a holistic view of the business.
Insight Delivery: Deliver structured, actionable insights from a cross-channel perspective — challenging the status quo and surfacing opportunities that others may overlook.
Senior Stakeholder Management: Manage alignment with functional leads and senior leadership, ensuring the team's analytical output translates into meaningful business actions and decisions.
Cross-Functional Project Leadership: Lead a diverse portfolio of strategic projects spanning multiple business units, driving execution from concept through impact measurement.
Data-Driven Strategy: Partner with Data Analysts and Scientists to convert data insights into holistic marketing strategies, maximizing business value.



What you'll need to thrive:



Experience: 3+ years of relevant experience in strategic, financial, or analytical roles within a digital/tech environment.
Education: A Bachelor's degree or higher in Business Administration, Finance, Economics, Econometrics, Statistics, or similar.
Business Acumen: Deep understanding of how diverse user acquisition strategies, channel economics, and marketing investments drive revenue and contribution margin growth in a test-and-learn framework.
Stakeholder Management: Proven ability to build trust and influence with senior leaders and cross-functional partners, driving alignment and converting analytical output into business action.
Data-Driven Decision Making: Proficiency in using a data-driven approach to identify business opportunities and make strategic decisions.
Strategic Mindset: Ability to operate at both the macro and micro level — zooming out to see full business implications while diving deep into the details when needed.
Analytical Skills: Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
Data Storytelling: Excellent data storytelling skills with compelling and easily understandable narratives for a wide range of audiences, including executives.



Stand out with:



Financial Fluency: Strong command of financial modeling, planning, and analysis — including P&L management, scenario modeling, and marketing spend optimization. Ability to translate financial data into actionable strategic recommendations.
Project Management: Excellent project management skills, particularly in agile methodologies.
Experience with Digital Marketing Channels: Familiarity with various digital marketing channels and strategies.
Proactive Ownership: High level of motivation, structured working style, and a track record of independently driving initiatives from idea to impact.



Worried about missing a few requirements? Still apply, and express your motivation as you may just be the right candidate for this or other roles!

Recruiting process for this role:



One-way interview
Call with recruiter
Case Study Presentation + technical interview
Interview with Stakeholders from marketing



What you can look forward to:

As you grow, so do we.


This is why at trivago, we prioritize your development, offer personalized coaching through Nilo, and provide workshops, educational meetups, conferences, free online learning courses, and access to a fully-equipped campus library.

Moving to join us?


No problem. You can count on the visa support from our talent support team, a relocation package, interest-free newcomer loan, free language classes, regular team and company-wide events to build experiences together.

Life happens.


We offer self-determined vacation (with a minimum of 25 vacation days), flexible working hours, up to 2 work from home days weekly. Additionally you can work remotely from a different location, within Germany or selected countries abroad for up to 20 days per year. You also get free access to the Heycare platform for personalized nanny assistance, and an on-campus kids room.

Enjoy your office days.


Use your daily canteen budget to share lunch with colleagues in our canteen, help yourself to complimentary snacks and drinks in our kitchens, choose from a variety of fitness options with our on-site gym, sports classes, and Urban Sports Club membership, and enjoy the comfort of ergonomic desks and focused work areas.

Thank you for considering a career at trivago! Our commitment to fostering an inclusive and enriching environment for all talents is at the heart of what we do. We understand that embarking on a new job opportunity is a blend of excitement and curiosity. Should any questions arise before you apply, feel free to reach out to us at joinus@trivago.com. Join us in our mission to make a positive impact on global travel, we look forward to your application!

description_full:
Company Description

It’s not just about your career… or your job title…it’s about who you are and the impact you are going to make on the world. Do you want to go into uncharted waters…do things that haven’t been done to make yours and someone else's life better? Wabtec has been doing that for decades and we will continue to do so! Whether it’s for each other or our customers, we put always People First.

Job Description

Who will you be working with?

You will be part of the global Friction & Rubber Group within Wabtec Corporation, a leading provider of friction and elastomer solutions for the rail, industrial, and powersports industries. The team operates in an international Product Line environment, collaborating closely with Sales, Engineering, and Communications functions. Together, you will support the delivery of innovative, sustainability‑focused solutions, including Green and Blue Friction technologies, used by major rail operators and manufacturers worldwide.

How will you make a difference?

As a Marketing Specialist, you will support and partially drive global marketing activities for the Friction & Rubber product portfolio. You will contribute to product positioning, value propositions, and go‑to‑market strategies in collaboration with cross‑functional and international teams. Your role will focus on developing marketing materials, supporting product launches, and strengthening communication across the Product Line. You will work closely with key stakeholders to enhance market visibility and support business growth in a highly technical and sustainability‑driven environment.

What will your typical day look like?


Support and help drive global marketing initiatives for the Product Line
Contribute to value propositions, product positioning, and key messaging
Create and maintain marketing and sales enablement materials
Support product launches and portfolio communication activities
Conduct market and competitor analyses
Support trade fairs, customer events, and internal marketing initiatives
Collaborate with cross‑functional and international teams


Qualifications

What do we want to know about you?


Degree in Marketing, Business, Engineering, or a related field
Several years of professional experience in marketing (B2B or industrial environment preferred)
Strong interest in technology, mobility, and sustainability topics
Structured and proactive working style with strong communication skills
Experience working in cross‑functional and/or international teams is a plus
Fluent English required


Additional Information

Legal authorization to work in EU is required. We will not sponsor individuals for employment visas, now or in the future, for this job opening.

Please note that we do not accept agency candidate submissions without a formalized agreement with Talent Acquisition. If you are an agency/third party Recruiter and have not been engaged by Wabtec for this search, we do not owe you a fee if you submit a candidate to the applicant tracking system that is ultimately hired.

What could you accomplish in a place that puts People First?

At Wabtec, it’s not just about a job - it’s about the impact you make. When our people come together, we’re Expanding the Possible by continuously improving what we do and how we do it - for our clients and each other.

If you’re ready to revolutionize how the world moves for future generations, Wabtec is the place for you.

Who are we?

Wabtec is a leading global provider of equipment, systems, digital solutions, and value-added services for the freight and transit rail sectors. Drawing on more than 150 years of experience, we are leading the way in safety, efficiency, reliability, innovation, and productivity. Whether it’s freight, transit, ports, logistics, mining, industrial, or marine, our expertise, technologies, and people together – are accelerating the future of transportation. With roots that date back to George Westinghouse, Thomas Edison, and Louis Faiveley, Wabtec has always built technologies and implemented solutions for a variety of sectors that are critical to meeting the needs of customers and governments alike.

Our global team of about 30,000 employees worldwide delivers performance that moves the world forward. We’re lifelong learners, obsessed with better. Learn more at www.WabtecCorp.com.

Culture powers us and the possibilities.

We believe the best ideas come from a mix of experiences and backgrounds. At Wabtec, we strive every day to create a place where everyone belongs. We’re building a culture where leadership, inclusion and your unique perspective fuel progress.

We’re proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer. We welcome talent of all backgrounds, experiences, and identities, including race, gender, age, disability, veteran status and more.

Need accommodation? Just let us know - we’ve got you.

You develop and execute personalization strategies to tailor content and experiences that boost user engagement and conversion





YOUR SKILLS MATTER


Experience You bring several years of product management experience, are skilled in agile methodologies, and have worked cross-functionally, especially on customer-facing digital products
Teamwork You have collaborated closely with engineering teams to define, prioritize, and deliver product features aligned with business goals
Skills You are adept at using data insights and A/B testing frameworks to validate hypotheses, optimize features, and drive product decisions
Quality of work You are energetic, hard-working, and continuously learning in a fast-changing digital ecosystem, focused on delivering the highest quality in complex products
Mentality & Problem Solving You have a hands-on, can-do attitude, are resourceful and creative, and capable of independently solving challenging problems
Communication You excel at communicating complex topics clearly and concisely across all teams





WHAT WE OFFER


Extra benefits Enjoy discounts on SIXT rent, share, ride and SIXT+, attractive vehicle leasing offers, and exclusive deals with partners for travel, tech, fashion and more

Our mobility platform ONE combines our products SIXT rent (car rental), SIXT share (car sharing), SIXT ride (taxi, ride, and chauffeur services), and SIXT+ (car subscription), giving our customers access to our fleet of 350,000 vehicles, the services of 4,000 cooperation partners, and around 5 million drivers worldwide. Together with our franchise partners, we are present in more than 110 countries at 2,000 rental stations. At SIXT, top-tier customer experience and outstanding customer service are our highest priorities. We believe in true entrepreneurship and long-term stability and align our corporate strategy with foresight. Get started with us and apply now!

Strong analytical, problem‑solving, and data interpretation skills.

description_full:
Join Scrive and be part of an international, fast-growing company headquartered in Stockholm, Sweden. Collaborate with smart, caring, and driven colleagues across Europe as we shape the Scrive story. 🚀




The opportunity

We are currently looking for a Product Manager to join our team and play a key role in shaping the future of digital identity across Europe. At Scrive we offer a vibrant culture, opportunities for growth, and the chance to make a real impact in the rapidly evolving eSignature industry.




This is a uniquely broad product role — one that will grow not just your product management craft, but also your business acumen. You'll sharpen your technical and project management skills, develop the communication chops to navigate difficult business decisions, and help shape the strategies behind them.




This is an exciting opportunity to drive the growth and evolution of our Identity product portfolio (the enablement part of our portfolio) and build products that our customers love and trust.




📍 The role is ideally based in any of Scrive’s hubs (Stockholm, Amsterdam, Oslo, Berlin, or Copenhagen), but are also open for remote work. You will report to the Group Product Manager for Identity and Trust who is based in Stockholm.




As a Product Manager, you will:

Own the product vision and strategic roadmapping: shape the roadmap for your product area, balancing short-term delivery with long-term strategy, while ensuring alignment with customer needs and market trends.
Drive product outcomes and delivery: define success metrics for functionalities and collaborate with cross-functional teams to deliver impactful, scalable solutions.
Collaborate in a product trio: work closely with Engineering, other Product Managers, and Product Design to ensure seamless delivery and adoption, while acting as a customer champion by gathering user feedback and competitor insights.
Lead commercial strategy and external partnerships: develop compelling pricing and Go-To-Market strategies to unlock growth, while building strong relationships with identity providers, integration partners, and key customers.




What we’re looking for

To succeed in this role, you should have:

2-4 years of experience in a Product Manager role, with a track record of leading complex SaaS products or services.
Proven track record in product development, roadmap planning, and translating customer needs into actionable features - preferably within a regulated or high-compliance industry.
Problem-solving skills: ability to navigate ambiguity with a proactive and scalable approach.
Excellent communication skills: strong relationship-building, collaboration, and storytelling abilities, with fluency in English.




🌟 Bonus points if you have:

Knowledge or experience working with electronic signatures and digital identity.
Experience in scaling products within a high-growth SaaS environment.
A background in building or scaling API-driven products where developers and technical teams are key users.
The ability to work from our offices in Stockholm, Copenhagen, Amsterdam or Berlin.




In this role, we see that you demonstrate strong skills in Discovery, Stakeholder communication, User empathy and Execution. These skills will play a key role in our assessment and discussions during the recruitment process.




Don’t meet every single requirement? Apply anyway! Studies show that women and people from underrepresented groups are less likely to apply for jobs unless they meet every qualification. At Scrive, we value diverse perspectives and encourage anyone who is excited about this role to apply.

PUMA is one of the world’s leading sports brands, designing, developing, and selling footwear, apparel and accessories. Founded in 1948, PUMA helps the world’s best athletes and teams perform at their best with its innovative products. Known for its iconic cat logo and the Formstrip, the company offers performance products in categories such as Football, Running and Training. Its Sportstyle collections are rooted in sports and inspire consumers by celebrating sports culture. With its long history and strong heritage, PUMA is proud of having one of the strongest archives in the industry, with many iconic products such as the Suede and the Speedcat. The PUMA Group owns the brands PUMA, Cobra Golf and stichd. The company distributes its products in over 120 countries, employs more than 20,000 people and is headquartered in Herzogenaurach/Germany. For more information, please visit https://about.puma.com.

Commitments

Career growth and learning

At PUMA, talent development is central to our strategy, aligning employee growth with business needs through regular evaluations, feedback, and personalized development plans. We leverage tools like Workday, Job Alert, and Talent Marketplace to support internal mobility, skills tracking, and succession planning. Our onboarding process fosters quick integration, while our self-directed learning culture is supported by over 23,000 online courses, localized content, language training via Busuu, and global e-learning campaigns. Key programs include Agile Learning, ILP/ILP²/ILP Focus, the Leadership Expedition, Speed Up for high potentials, and Future Talent programs like apprenticeships, dual studies, and internships. Our internal training department ensures flexible, needs-based learning and is exploring new evaluation methods. Coaching for senior staff, CAT Talks for knowledge sharing, and specialized retail development programs through PUMA University empower employees at all levels to grow, lead, and succeed in a dynamic environment.
… more

Work-life balance

We offer a wide range of services and benefits to support employee health and wellbeing. Our wellbeing strategy focuses on four key areas: Flex, Social, Finance and Athlete. In 2024, we continued providing sports classes, indoor and outdoor training facilities, gym sessions, and sports events. Our “Be Well Weeks” offered health checks, nutritional counselling, ergonomic support, and mental health resources. To support work-life balance, we offer flexible working models, including mobile office, part-time roles, and sabbaticals. All global offices operate under a hybrid model, giving employees flexibility in work hours and location. Since 2015, our Headquarters has held the German “audit berufundfamilie” certificate, recognizing our family-friendly environment. This includes parent-child offices, nursing rooms, childcare facilities, and summer camps during school holidays - ensuring a supportive workplace for working parents and promoting overall employee wellbeing.
… more

Diversity, equity, and inclusion

Creating a fair and inclusive workplace is central to how we operate. With 145 nationalities across our global team, we value the diversity that strengthens our culture and performance. Among our leaders worldwide, 42% are female, 58% male, and <1% identify as diverse. We prohibit all forms of discrimination and are committed to equal opportunities in career development and compensation. From 2020 to 2024, the Financial Times ranked PUMA as a “Leader in Diversity” among top European companies - five years in a row. In 2024, we updated our DEI policy, launched new learning tracks, introduced employee resource groups like Juntos, and marked Pride Month with dedicated events. We support employees with disabilities by adapting workspaces and providing representation through elected councils. FPI Fair Pay Innovation Lab, a German NGO, supports us in analysing and improving our pay strategies. We assess roles using a gender-neutral, job-focused approach to ensure fair, transparent, and standardised compensation for all employees. PUMA is certified as a Fair Pay Developer and Leader in several countries, ensuring compensation is based on skills and performance - not gender or background.
… more

Social impact

We know that sport is one of the strongest links between people – we feel it every day. And so, we readily share our Speed & Spirit by engaging with local communities all around the globe. Since launching our community engagement program in 2016, PUMA employees have contributed over 240,000 hours globally. These efforts span four key categories: Sports & Health, Protection of Environment, Equality & Non-discrimination, and Education. Activities include beach clean-ups, tree planting, charity runs, and initiatives supporting underprivileged communities—especially children—through food donations and school supplies.
… more

Environmental sustainability

As a sports brand, we know how to push ourselves to be better. We approach sustainability in much the same way. At PUMA, we integrate sustainability into every aspect of our manufacturing processes – from sourcing raw materials to production, both environmentally and socially. Much like an athlete's training programme, we have set ambitious goals and are committed to compliance and minimising our impact on the planet.
